structured.summary: The paper introduces DocPrompting, a novel approach for natural-language-to-code generation that leverages code documentation retrieval to improve code generation models' ability to handle unseen functions and libraries.

strengths.point: The paper addresses a significant limitation of existing code generation models by enabling them to generalize to unseen libraries and functions.
DocPrompting is demonstrated to improve performance on established benchmarks, showing a relative gain in pass@1 and pass@10 metrics.
The approach is general and can be applied to any programming language and underlying neural model.
The authors introduce new benchmarks and datasets, contributing valuable resources for future research.

weaknesses.point: The paper lacks a detailed comparison with other retrieval-based code generation methods.
weaknesses.evidence: Related Work section is not detailed in the provided text.

weaknesses.point: The computational efficiency of the retrieval and generation process is not thoroughly discussed.
weaknesses.evidence: Methodology section does not provide detailed computational analysis.

weaknesses.point: The paper does not explore the potential limitations of relying on documentation, such as outdated or incomplete docs.
weaknesses.evidence: Discussion and Limitations sections are not detailed in the provided text.

weaknesses.point: The evaluation primarily focuses on Python and Bash, which may limit the generalizability of the results to other programming languages.
The paper does not provide a user study or qualitative analysis of the generated code's usability.
weaknesses.evidence: Results section focuses on quantitative metrics.

questions.question: How does DocPrompting handle outdated or incomplete documentation?
questions.motivation: Understanding the robustness of the approach in real-world scenarios where documentation may not always be reliable.

questions.question: What are the computational requirements for the retrieval and generation process in DocPrompting?
questions.motivation: Assessing the feasibility of deploying this approach in resource-constrained environments.

questions.question: Can the authors provide more details on how the new benchmarks were constructed and validated?
questions.motivation: Ensuring the reliability and relevance of the new datasets introduced in the paper.
